ops.title: ccx pack read-only approvers + two ccx vulns the review found (git-show flag-injection file clobber, web link-local/metadata SSRF)
ops.body:
Shipped the ccx capt-hook pack's read-only **approvers** (auto-approve the read-only ccx surface so the guard rewrites don't then hit a permission dialog) plus two real ccx vulnerabilities the adversarial security review surfaced. Two commits on `main` (2026-07-12): `fix(vcs,web): block git-show ref flag-injection and cloud-metadata SSRF` then `feat(hooks): auto-approve the read-only ccx surface in the ccx pack`. Pack bumped 0.4.0 → 0.5.0 in both `plugin/hooks/capt-hook.toml` and `.claude/capt-hook.toml` (CI `manifests` job enforces lockstep).

**The approvers** (`plugin/hooks/approval_guards.py`, capt-hook `approve()` on `Event.PermissionRequest`, published in capt-hook ≥9.0.0):
- MCP: server-pinned exact-name allowlist — split `evt.tool_name` on `__` (maxsplit 2), require server ∈ {cc-context, plugin_cc-context_cc-context} AND tool ∈ the 14 read-only names. Bare `Tool("ccx_code_grep")` is WRONG (suffix-matches any server). `ccx_code_replace` preview approved only when `not evt.input.raw.get("apply")` (`evt.input.raw`, not private `evt.tool_input`).
- CLI (Bash): fail-closed order — raw-text `UNSAFE_EXPANSION = [`${]|<\(|>\(` scan on `cl.raw` FIRST (the parser silently drops unquoted `$(…)` from argv, so structural checks are blind), then `is_single_command`+`is_plain_argv` (common.py), then executable literally `ccx` or `ccx_bin()` (not a Path-name match), then literal (family, op) allowlist, then reject bare `--` token. captain-hook's general `ReadOnlyCommand` was reverted as "unsound against shell expansion" — this survives only as a closed literal set for one binary.

**Vuln 1 — git-show ref flag-injection (arbitrary file clobber), confirmed+reproduced.** `internal/vcs/show.go` `showGit` passed `ref` to `git show` unvalidated, so `ccx vcs show -- --output=/tmp/x` reached git as an output flag and clobbered the file (the `--` smuggles it past cobra as the positional ref). Fixed by inserting `--end-of-options` before `ref` (NOT `--`, which demotes ref to a pathspec and breaks `HEAD~3`). `vcs diff`/`history` were already safe via rev-parse validation; only `show` was exposed. `showJJ` was safe (routes through `gitCommitID`).

**Vuln 2 — web link-local/cloud-metadata SSRF.** `internal/web/fetch.go` routed ALL local targets to plainHTTP, so `ccx web read http://169.254.169.254/…` (or `metadata.google.internal`) got fetched. Fixed: `ErrLinkLocalRefused` sentinel + `linkLocalTarget` (zone-stripped ParseIP, so `fe80::1%en0` counts) + `classifyHost` now resolves by-name-local hosts too and refuses on ANY link-local answer. Loopback/private/split-horizon stay allowed. One shared classifier feeds all gates (fetch/renderFetch/redirect/browser). User chose "harden ccx fetch" over dropping web from the approver.

**Process notes for next time:**
- codex (gpt-5.6-sol) REFUSES adversarial-security-review prompts ("attacker/bypass/audit" framing) with an OpenAI cybersecurity content-filter error — happened on the refuter AND verify passes. Escalate that lane to **fable** (Claude does authorized first-party defensive review fine). See [[token-wastage-audit-2026-07]] lane routing.
- The repo `.venv` carries a broken editable captain-hook (`ModuleNotFoundError: cc_transcript.command`) → the verbatim `PYTHONPATH=plugin uv run … --with capt-hook` pytest command FALSE-FAILS locally; run with `env -u VIRTUAL_ENV` + absolute `PYTHONPATH=<repo>/plugin` so `--with capt-hook` resolves the published wheel. Inverts the usual [[capt-hook-local-testing]] "uvx = false green" — here the editable is the false-failure.
- e2e proof: pipe PermissionRequest JSON into `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks run PermissionRequest`; an allow prints `decision:{behavior:"allow"}`, a withheld one prints nothing.
